description: CCQS comparison after cad-quality-v2 improvements vs AI reference floor plan

baseline_scores.note: Scores from main branch before this session (from scores.json pre-commit)

vq_issues: Kitchen dimensions text appears quite large
Living/Hall dimensions text orientation and size could be better proportioned
Some door swing arcs overlap with wall lines in tight spaces
Minor alignment inconsistencies in dimension line placement
Room dimension text in Kitchen and Dining appears slightly cramped
Setback annotations could be positioned slightly cleaner
UP arrow and staircase representation could be more detailed

ai_reference.note: AI-generated image PDF lacks parseable dimension text, ft-in labels, and area schedules — scores 0 on 3 of 5 automated metrics despite strong visual quality

improvements_shipped: DXF: DEFPOINTS layer created at doc init (non-plotting)
DXF: ARCH_MM dimstyle with architectural tick arrow (archtick)
DXF: Wall hatches now solid fill (was silently dropped due to 'elevation' float bug)
DXF: logger.warning() instead of silent except:pass on 4 geometry error paths
Shapely: geom_type string dispatch replaces hasattr(poly, 'geoms') duck-typing
Shapely: cap_style/join_style integer form replaced with string literals
PDF: Room label font cap raised 10pt -> 12pt with tighter size scaling
PDF: Staircase UP arrow larger (thicker stem, bigger head), 'UP' text above tip
PDF: North arrow radius 12->16pt, repositioned into top-right margin above plot

analysis.vs_ai_reference: PlanForge v2 outperforms AI reference by +59 points (97 vs 36.83). AI reference scores well on visual quality (17/20) but produces no machine-readable text, failing dimension_density, ft_in_labels, and layout_completeness entirely.
analysis.vs_baseline: Approval PDF improved +1 point (96->97) on visual quality. Standard PDF held at 96. Key structural bug fixed: wall hatches were silently dropped on all previous DXF exports — now correctly solid-fill.
analysis.remaining_vq_gaps: Dimension text size in kitchen/dining too prominent relative to room size. Door swing arcs occasionally overlap walls in tight spaces. These require layout-aware label sizing.
